subject: UX & Research
title: Retention and Churn
description: A short lesson to help you apply Retention and Churn.

hook.text: You gain 1,000 users monthly but lose 950. Growth looks great until you check the leak at the bottom.

buildup.text: Churn rate = users who leave ÷ total users. Retention is the inverse. Both measure the same leaky bucket.

discovery.text: Bain found that a 5% increase in retention boosts profits 25-95%. Keeping users is cheaper than acquiring them.

twist.text: Day-1 retention differs from day-30. Early churn is onboarding failure. Late churn is value failure.

climax.text: Segment churn by cohort and behavior. Users who complete onboarding retain 3x better. Fix the first week.

punchline.text: Retention beats acquisition. Fix the first week to stop early churn.

quiz.question: What does a 5% increase in customer retention do to profits?
options: Increases profits by 25-95% according to Bain research
Has no measurable impact on profits
